item.title: 如果动物会编码会怎样…… | What If Animals Could Code...
item.description:
好的，我们又来了！ 在本系列的最后一篇文章中，您提出了惊人的 40 条关于代码名人的搞笑评论。 我期待看到你们这一次的天才们会想出什么来。

那么今天的主题就到这里了。 当 YouTube 出现故障时，训练有素的猴子会联合起来尽快解决问题。 猴子可能是唯一会编码的非人类动物，但如果其他动物是开发者，它们会怎么做？


我相信他们，他们为什么要撒谎？

发挥您的想象力，并就聘请动物作为开发人员的有趣建议发表评论。 这是我的一些建议，如果你愿意，可以随意重复使用相同的动物。

  1. 熊猫- 将是熊猫程序员！
  2. 蜗牛——总是在家工作
  3. 蛇——我需要提到 Python 吗？
  4. 虫子——我有一种感觉，他们很难找到一个需要他们的工作场所
  5. 奶牛- 他们更喜欢硬件，他们遵循 mooooores 法律
  6. 狮子- 初创公司的强大合作伙伴，但希望他们获得最大的利润份额
  7. 树懒- 使用 Copilot 可能会受益
  8. 绵羊- 可能是要租用的绵羊，但值得吗？
  9. 猫——不会碰代码，它们会向经理推销自己
  10. 猪- 脏代码，但它们实际上非常聪明
  11. 鼹鼠- 他们会喜欢 C#
  12. 蝙蝠- 只要您可以将屏幕倒置，它们可能值得雇用
  13. 狗- 可能是他们最好的。 天。 曾经。
  14. 鲸鱼- 雇用一个，你的老板会打电话给你谈论办公费用


对不起考拉先生，但你总是做最少的工作

安东·古斯塔夫松—— 一旦暂停，perssondennis 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，perssondennis 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，perssondennis 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，并且只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 perssondennis 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 perssondennis：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记的 perssondennis 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

1 Top 10 Celebrities Who Code 2 What If Animals Could Code...

Okay, here we go again! In the last article in this series you came up with amazingly 40 hilarious comments on the topic celebrities who code. I look forward to see what you geniuses may come up with this time.

So here's today's topic. When YouTube goes down, highly trained monkeys join up to fix the problem as fast as possible. Monkeys may be the only non-human animal that codes, but what would other animals do if they were developers?


I believe them, why would they lie?

Use your imagination and comment funny suggestions of what it could be like to hire an animal as a developer. Here's some suggestions of mine, feel free to reuse the same animals if you want to.

  1. Pandas - would be pandastic programmers!
  2. Snails - would always be working from home
  3. Snakes - do I need to mention Python?
  4. Bugs - I have a feeling they would struggle finding a workplace who wants them
  5. Cows - they are more into hardware, they follow mooooores law
  6. Lions - strong partners for a startup, but expect them to take the lion share of the profit
  7. Sloths - would probably benefit using Copilot
  8. Sheep - may be sheep to hire, but is it worth it?
  9. Cats - wouldn't touch code, they would promote themselves to managers
  10. Pigs - dirty code, but they are actually pretty clever
  11. Moles - they would love to C#
  12. Bats - as long as you are fine with putting your screen upside down, they could maybe be worth hiring
  13. Dogs - would probably be their best. day. ever.
  14. Whales - hire one and you boss will call you in to talk about office expenses


I'm sorry Mr. Koala, but you always do the bear minimum of work

1 Top 10 Celebrities Who Code 2 What If Animals Could Code...

Anton Gustafsson - Jul 23

Marcel Scognamiglio - Aug 4

item.title: 显示 Flutter 自定义错误消息 | Showing Flutter custom error messages
item.description:
错误处理是一个复杂的过程。 这很无聊，需要很多时间。 问题之一是在 Flutter 应用程序中向用户显示错误。 今天我们就来聊聊这个。

所有项目都使用不同的方式向用户显示异常和错误信息。

有时应用程序会显示常见的小吃店

有时它通过模态对话框实现

有时它使用横幅屏幕

但我认为这不是问题。
我们都是很酷的开发人员，并且知道如何在我们的应用程序的 UI 中制作任何东西。

当我们需要了解哪些消息以及我们想要显示的位置时，问题就开始了。 而最重要的问题是如何以简单的方式为应用程序的所有服务和存储库处理它们

为此，您可以使用talker library。
如何为我在本文中展示的应用程序配置它

但是今天我们要谈点别的。
我们需要一种简单的方法来设置显示一次的错误消息，而不是一直在应用程序中复制此代码。

您可以在项目存储库中查看完整示例。

您可以检查talker_shop_app_example应用程序示例，使用BLoC 作为状态管理和由talker 显示的调整异常

定制

但是每个人都想使用不同的小部件来显示错误。
而这点可以使用talker_flutter库来解决。

为了自定义snackbars，你可以使用TalkerWrapper的选项

class TalkerWrapperOptions { final String exceptionTitle ; final String errorTitle ; final TalkerExceptionBuilder ? exceptionAlertBuilder ; final TalkerErrorBuilder ? errorAlertBuilder ; final bool enableErrorAlerts ; final bool enableExceptionAlerts ; }
  • 使用exceptionAlertBuilder和errorAlertBuilder在快餐栏中构建自定义小部件。
  • 使用enableErrorAlerts和enableExceptionAlerts过滤小吃吧。
  • 将exceptionTitle和errorTitle用于自定义小吃店标题。

更多定制

如果你想显示其他小部件（除了 Snackbars）——你可以使用 TalkerListener 而不是 TalkerWrapper。

 TalkerListener ( talker: talker , listener: ( data ) { /// Show your error messages on modal dialogs, screens, etc }, child: /// Your screen or app widget, );

结论

我希望我设法在文章中解释了我想要的一切。
感谢您阅读这个小帖子🙏！

在 GitHub 上与我联系，请为talker包添加✨star✨
如果您尝试在您的应用程序中使用它，我将非常高兴



原文:

Error handling is a complex process. It's very boring and takes a lot of time. One of the problems is showing errors to the user in the Flutter application. Today we will talk about this.

All projects use different ways to showing exceptions and errors information to user.

Sometimes applications show common snackbars

Sometimes it realized with modal dialogs

Sometimes it make with banner screens

But I thinks that's not a problem.
We are all cool developers and know how to make any things in UI of our applications.

The problem starts at the moment when we need to understand which messages and where we want to show. And the most important problem is how to process them in a simple way for all services and repositories of the application

Just for this, you can use the talker library.
How to configure it for the application I showed in this article

But today we will talk about something else.
We need a simple way to set up the error messages showing once and not copy this code in the application all the time.

😎 Let's do this...

1) Add talker_flutter dependency in pubspec.yaml

2) Init talker for your application

void main() { final talker = Talker( loggerSettings: TalkerLoggerSettings( enableColors: !Platform.isIOS, ), ); runZonedGuarded( () => runApp( CustomErrorMessagesExample(talker: talker), ), (Object error, StackTrace stack) { talker.handle(error, stack, 'Uncaught app exception'); }, );
}

3) You need to implement wrapper at initial route of your application or at screen which where you want to show error messages.

@override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return Scaffold( body: TalkerWrapper( talker: talker, options: const TalkerWrapperOptions( enableErrorAlerts: true, ), child: const Center(child: Text('Your screen')), ), ); }

You can see the full example in the project repository.

And you can check talker_shop_app_example application example with BLoC as state management and tuned exceptions showing by talker

Customization

But everyone wants to use different widgets to display errors.
And this point can be solved using the talker_flutter library.

In order to customize snackbars, you can use options of TalkerWrapper

class TalkerWrapperOptions { final String exceptionTitle; final String errorTitle; final TalkerExceptionBuilder? exceptionAlertBuilder; final TalkerErrorBuilder? errorAlertBuilder; final bool enableErrorAlerts; final bool enableExceptionAlerts;
}
  • Use exceptionAlertBuilder and errorAlertBuilder for build custom widgets in snackbars.
  • Use enableErrorAlerts and enableExceptionAlerts for filtering snackbars.
  • Use exceptionTitle and errorTitle for custom snackbar titles.

More customization

And if you want to show other widgets (other than Snackbars) - you can use TalkerListener instead of TalkerWrapper.

TalkerListener( talker: talker, listener: (data) { /// Show your error messages on modal dialogs, screens, etc }, child: /// Your screen or app widget,
);

Conclusion

I hope I managed to explain everything I wanted in the article.
Thank you for reading this small post🙏!

Connect with me on GitHub and pls put ✨star✨ for talker package
I will be very pleased if you try to use it in your application
item.author: frezyx

item.title: 我计划如何使用编码作为工具来拯救我的未来 | How I Plan to use coding as a tool to save my future
item.description:
维托梅德勒

发表于现在，我们都看到了这个写着“Will Code For Food”的模因，并认为这只是一个笑话。 您几乎不知道在贫穷国家人们确实会这样做，今天我将讨论我如何为食物编码以求生存。

据谷歌称，美国的前端开发人员每年的收入约为 105,344 美元，因此每个月的税前收入约为 8700 美元。 哇，这很好，但在黎巴嫩，每月超过 290 万磅。 耶稣！ 你会如此轻易地成为亿万富翁！ 现在等一下，以今天的汇率计算，每天工作 8 小时，每月可赚 96.6 美元。

可怕？ 我知道。 那么，我到底为什么要花几年的时间来学习你问的编码呢？ 就像刚毕业的大学毕业生乐于以每月 120 美元的薪水安顿下来一样。 说到大学，我可以选择每年支付 5000 美元（想象一下，如果你的收入为 100 美元/平方米，那就是 5 年的工作），去私立大学或公立大学，因为他们不时停课 3 个月以上贫困。

现在想象一下你自己在我的鞋子里，你去健身房实现你梦想的身体，只是发现你每次买肉或鸡肉都会花费你月薪的 10%。 你没有动力，决定看场电影，稍等片刻，你在黎巴嫩， 4 个月没见过政府的电了。 一个人可能会失去希望并抱怨，但什么时候会奏效呢？ 这是一个起床或放弃的时刻，我决定寻找解决方案。

我在这里，日夜工作在个人项目上，我知道这会让我到达我想去的地方，学习编码是我 17 岁时做出的最好决定，我确信它会让我免于像许多人一样挨饿我的国家是。

感谢您的准备，如果您对我写的任何主题感兴趣，请发表评论并告诉我。

这是我的投资组合网站，供有兴趣的人使用。
文件夹

一旦暂停，vitomedlej 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，vitomedlej 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，vitomedlej 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 vitomedlej 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 vitomedlej：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 vitomedlej 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

VitoMedlej

Posted on Aug 7

Now, we've all seen this meme that says "Will Code For Food", and thought it's just that, a joke. Little did you know that people literally do that in poor countries, and today I will talk about how I aim to code for food in order to survive.

According to Google, a frontend dev in the US earns about $105,344 per year, so about 8700$ per month pretaxed. wow that's good you'd say but here in Lebanon, it's over 2.9 million LBP per month. Jesus! you'd become a billionaire so essily! Now hold on a minute, at today's exchange rate, that's a bloody 96.6$ per month working 8 hours per day.

Horrible? I know. So why the hell would I spend years of my life learning to code you ask? Just like fresh college graduates are happy to settle with 120$ salary per month. Speaking of college, I had the choice of paying 5000$ per year (imagine if you earn 100$/m, that's 5 years of work) for a private University or public one where they shutdown for 3+ month every now and then because of poverty.

Now imagine your self in my shoes for a moment, you go to the gym to achieve your dream body, just to find out each time you buy meat or chicken it'll cost you 10% of your monthly salary. You get unmotivated and decide to watch a movie, hold on a second, you're in Lebanon, Haven't seen the government's electricity in 4 month. One might lose hope and complain but when does that ever work? It was a get up or give up moment, I decided to look for solutions.

Here I am, Working day and night on personal projects I know will get me where I want to be, Learning to code was the best decision I took as a 17 year old, and im certain it'll save me from starving like many in my country are.

Thank you for ready and if you are interested in any topic I wrote about leave a comment and let me know.

Here is my portfolio website for those who are interested.
Portfolio

item.title: 如何从任何地方编码 | How to Code From Anywhere
item.description:
在这篇文章中，我将总结从任何地方开始编码所需的一切。 这可能会让您感到惊讶，但您不需要昂贵的笔记本电脑和良好的互联网连接来学习如何编码。 这是你真正需要的：

对于计算设备，我指的是能够进行足够 FLOPS（每秒浮点运算）以安装 Linux 操作系统的任何智能手机、计算机或笔记本电脑。 任何像摩托罗拉这样至少有 4GB 内存的手机都绰绰有余。 当然，笔记本电脑更容易，但并不是每个人都有钱买得起。 如果您从手机开始，请确保还获得能够通过 USB C 端口或蓝牙连接的带鼠标的键盘。 除此之外，拥有一个可以连接的便携式屏幕也很有用（这主要是通过 USB C 完成的，因为蓝牙不会传输太多数据）。

全球有近 10 亿人（10 亿）没有电，这意味着大约 12% 的全球人口。 还有更多电力昂贵或不稳定的地方。 要学习如何从任何地方编码，您需要搬到有电的地方； 至少足以为您的计算设备充电。 如果电力不稳定，通常最好有一个有电池的计算设备，这样你就可以随时工作。

在世界上大多数地区，停电很常见。 如果您所在地区经常断电持续超过几个小时，那么最好购买一个能够为您的计算设备多次充电的移动电源，这样您就可以在几天内断电。 大多数手机都可以用非常便宜的移动电源充电，所以这可能是大多数人的最佳选择，但如果你能负担得起，拥有一个可以为笔记本电脑充电的笔记本电脑移动电源会很棒。 如果您的城镇没电了，这可能是一个活生生的救星！

如果你有我们的资料，你不需要有稳定的互联网连接，但最好每周至少有一次或两次，以便与其他程序员联系，查找特定的利基信息和库，以及下载某些资产。 您不需要快速的互联网连接，也不需要稳定。

如果您还希望能够与世界各地的潜在客户进行通话和/或视频通话，建议使用至少 6 Mbit 的稳定互联网连接。 如果在您所在的地区很难获得，您可以考虑前往更容易获得的地区，或者您可以尝试使用您拥有的互联网数量。

最重要的是您掌握了我们的软件、工具和学习资料。 它们专门针对像您这样没有足够财务能力购买昂贵物品并一直在线的人。

来自任何地方的代码提供多种大小的捆绑包（最小 2MB，最大 20GB），可以通过任何闪存驱动器下载或传输。 如果您获得了最小捆绑包（如果您的互联网连接非常慢），您已经可以了解有关全栈 typescript 的所有内容。 如果您获得更大的捆绑包，它还将包括音频和视频课程、一些程序等等。

开始编码总是有原因的。 可能是因为您想养家糊口，也可能是因为您想环游世界并获得自由。 但是你需要确保你专注于你的目的和动机，并决定致力于实现这一目标。 如果你在两周后退出，你就浪费了你的时间。 这不是短跑，而是马拉松。



原文:

In this post I will summarize all that is needed to start coding from anywhere. The Code From Anywhere movement aims to make it as cheap and easy as possible for developers from anywhere around the world to start coding from anywhere and gain freedom. It might surprise you, but you don't need an expensive laptop and a good internet connection to learn how to code. This is what you REALLY need:

1: A Computing Device

With a computing device I mean any smartphone, computer, or laptop that is able to make enough FLOPS (floating point operations per second) to install the Linux Operating System. Any phone like a Motorola with at least 4GB of RAM will be more than sufficient. Of course, a laptop is easier, but not everybody has the financials to afford one. In case you start with a phone, make sure to also get a keyboard with mouse that are able to connect, either through the USB C port or through bluetooth. Besides that it can be useful to have a portable screen that can be connected (this mostly is done through USB C, as bluetooth doesn't transfer so much data).

2: Electricity

There are almost a billion people (1000 million) without electricity in the world, which means about 12% of the global population. There are much more where electricity is expensive or unstable. To learn how to Code From Anywhere you need to move to an area where there is electricity available; at least enough to charge your computing device. If the electricity is not stable, it is usually better to have a computing device that has a battery so you can always work.

In most areas around the world, powercuts are common. If powercuts that last longer than a few hours are common in your area, it may be a good idea to get a powerbank that is able to charage your computing device multiple times so you can go without power for a couple of days. Most phones can be charged with very cheap power banks so this is probably the best option for most people, but if you can afford it, it can be great to have a laptop powerbank that can charage your laptop. This can be a live saver if your town runs out of power!

3: Internet Connection

If you have our material, you don't need to have a stable internet connection, but it's good to have it at least once or twice a week, in order to connect with other programmers, to look up specific niche information and libraries, and to download certain assets. You don't need a fast internet connection and it doesn't need to be stable.

If you want to also be able to make calls and/or videocalls with potential clietns around the world, a stable internet connection of at least 6 Mbit is recommended. If this is hard to obtain in your area, you could consider traveling to an area where it's easier to obtain, or you could just try to live with the amount of internet that you have.

4: Our software, tools and study materials

The most important is that you get hold of our software, tools and study materials. They are specifically targeted to people like you that don't have all the financials to buy expensive stuff and be online all the time.

Code from anywhere provides a bundle in multiple sizes (2MB minimum, 20 GB maximum) that can be downloaded or transfered over any flashdrive. If you get the minimum bundle (in case you have a very slow internet connection) you can already learn everything about full stack typescript. If you get the bigger bundle it will also include an audio and video course, some programs, and much more.

5: A strong will and purpose

There is always a reason to start coding. It might be because you want to provide for your family, or it might be because you wish to travel the world and be free. But you need to make sure you focus on your purpose and motivation and decidce to commit to achieving this. If you quit after two weeks, you have wasted your time. This is not a sprint but a marathon.
item.author: codefromanywhere42

item.title: 您需要了解的有关 HTML 输入类型的所有信息 | Everything you'll ever need to know about HTML Input Types
item.description:
几乎我们使用 HTML 开发的每个应用程序都在某处使用input ，但有一堆输入type您可能不知道。 因此，让我们看一下您可能不了解的有关input类型的所有内容。

常见的 HTML 输入类型

在 HTML 中，我们可以给输入一个特定的类型。 例如，一个简单的text输入，用户可以在框中输入文本，如下所示：

我们使用的最常见的输入类型是：

  • 以及这些基本类型：

    • 还有search - 虽然不常用，但它可以用于搜索输入字段，当您输入时会显示一个小十字。
    • 我们也有reset ，它创建了一个不提交表单的按钮，并说reset 。 它将重置所有其他表单元素。

    这些常见的input类型无处不在，它们看起来像这样：

    带有类型验证的基本 HTML 输入

    除了这些简单的类型之外，还有一些其他的非常有用的类型，例如：

    • 注意：这些输入类型确实在客户端验证输入，但不应将其用作确保输入仅包含该类型的方法。 例如， email仅接受电子邮件类型，但用户可以轻松更改前端以提交不是email的内容。 因此，请确保您也检查后端。

      日期 HTML 输入类型

      我们大多数人都熟悉type="date" ，但是有很多用于 HTML 输入的日期类型，包括：

      • 每一个都如下所示。 这些非常有用，尽管仅限于浏览器的 UI 实现：

        其他 HTML 输入类型

        除了所有这些，我们还有以下附加类型：

        • 输入的文件类型

          输入的文件类型创建一个文件上传接口：

          输入的图像类型

          这可能是您最不可能使用的一种。 它允许您为输入提供src和alt属性，用作表单的提交按钮 - 最终使您的输入像图像一样：

           <input type= "image" alt= "Login" src= "login-button.png" />

          结论

          这涵盖了您今天可以使用的每种HTML 输入类型。 我希望你学到了一些新东西，即使它只是古怪的type="image"输入类型。 如果您想了解更多关于 Web 开发的信息，可以在twitter上关注我，或者查看fjolt 。

一旦暂停，smpnjn 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，smpnjn 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，smpnjn 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 smpnjn 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 smpnjn 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Pretty much every application we develop using HTML uses input somewhere, but there's a bunch of input types you might not know about. So let's take a look at everything you might not know about input types.

Common HTML Input Types

In HTML, we can give an input a specific type. For example, a simple text input, where the user can type text into a box, looks like this:

The most common input types we use are:

  • hidden - a form input that is hidden, usually for storing values the user doesn't need to see
  • text - a simple text input.
  • password - an input where the text is starred out since it is a password.
  • submit - a submit button for submitting the form.
  • button - like a submit, but it doesn't submit the form!
  • radio - a button which can be selected, from a range of list items.
  • checkbox - a set of buttons which can be checked and unchecked.

As well as these basic types:

  • there is also search - although not as commonly used, it can be used for a search input field, which shows a little cross when you type into it.
  • and we also have reset, which creates a button which does not submit the form, and says reset. It will reset all other form elements.

These common input types are all over the place, and they look like this:

Basic HTML Inputs with Type Validation

As well as these simple types, there are a few others which can be quite useful, such as:

  • tel - for a telephone number.
  • url - validates that the input is a url.
  • email - validates that the input is an email.
  • number - validates that the input is a number.
  • range - validates that the input is a range.

Note: these input types do validate the input on the client side, but it should not be used as a method to ensure that an input only contains that type. For example, email only accepts an email type, but a user can easily change the frontend to submit something which is not an email. So make sure you check on the backend too.

Date HTML Input Types

Most of us are familiar with type="date", but there are a bunch of date types for HTML inputs, including:

  • date - any date input.
  • month - lets you select a single month.
  • week - lets you select a single week.
  • time - lets you select a specific time.
  • datetime-local - lets you select date/time combination.

Each of these are shown below. These are pretty useful, although limited to the browser's implementation of the UI:

Other HTML Input Types

As well as all of these, we also have the following additional types:

  • file - for uploading a file to the server.
  • image - the strangest of all, for graphical submit buttons!

file type for inputs

The file type for inputs creates a file upload interface:

image type for inputs

This is probably the one you are least likely to use. It lets you give an src and alt attribute to your input, which is used as the submit button for your form - ultimately making your input act like an image:

<input type="image" alt="Login" src="login-button.png" />

Conclusion

That covers every HTML input type you can use today. I hope you've learned something new, even if it's just the quirky type="image" input type. If you want to learn more about web development, you can follow me on twitter, or check out fjolt.

item.title: Como produzir eventos remotos | Como produzir eventos remotos
item.description:
莉萨·费雷拉（ Lissa Ferreira ）为Feministech

发表于嗯 eventto não é só aqueles grandes、cheios de Palestrantes、painelistas、e com uma super esttrutura。 包容性的dependo do que for, uma reunião entre amigues pode ser muito mais valiosa que um eventto grande que você não vai conseguir usar o que você aprendeu。

一个平台 exige 登录 ou não？

  • 哦，伟大的 dia chegou！ 做evento，e so bora！

    最终确定

    瓦利德·尼哈尔·艾哈迈德 - 一旦暂停，feministech 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

    • 一旦解除暂停，feministech 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦未发布，feministech 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 feministech 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

    感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记女性技术的方法：

    举报其他不当行为

    坚定不移的女性技术将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



    原文:

    Lissa Ferreira for Feministech

    Posted on Aug 7

    De forma geral, eventos são momentos onde pessoas se juntam para interagir, confraternizar, e ás vezes dividirem algum conhecimento.

    Um evento não é só aqueles grandes, cheios de palestrantes, painelistas, e com uma super estrutura. Uma reunião entre pessoas amigas já é um evento. Inclusive dependo do que for, uma reunião entre amigues pode ser muito mais valiosa que um evento grande que você não vai conseguir usar o que você aprendeu.

    Para o escopo desse artigo, vamos nos focar em eventos maiores, já com palestrantes, palco, transmissão,etc. feitos de forma remota.

    Para que fazer eventos?

    Eventos são espaços muito úteis para networking e troca de conhecimento, tanto com as pessoas participantes assistindo pessoas palestrantes ou painelistas, mas principalmente das pessoas participantes, COM outras pessoas participantes. É exatamente na interação das pessoas que estão participando do evento entre si que está a magia de evento. Caso isso não ocorra, o evento se torna apenas uma exposição de conteúdo. Que também é algo bom, mas que poderia ser muito melhor.

    1. Sobre o quê vai ser seu evento?

    Primeiro, o tema que será abordado no seu evento.

    • Será apenas técnico?
    • Terão palestras sobre softkills?
    • Será focado num tema específico, ou o tema será mais generalista?
    • Quando será feito?

    Faça essas perguntas com você, e as pessoas que estão te ajudando a organizar esse evento, e decida sobre o tema e a abrangência dele.

    2. Qual será o público alvo do seu evento?

    É importante definir o público alvo do seu evento para saber quais conteúdos serão vinculados sobre o assunto.

    Por exemplo, se o seu evento é direcionado à pessoas que estão iniciando no mundo da tecnologia, a linguagem e conteúdo do evento devem coincidir com esse público. Exatamente por isso que é importante que o público alvo seja bem definido.

    3. Como as pessoas vão poder participar do evento?

    Nessa etapa deve ser definida a forma que as pessoas vão poder participar do seu evento.

    • Ele será pago?
    • Terá uma página de inscrição ou só um link de acesso?
    • Terá um limite para quantas pessoas vão poder participar?

    Essas perguntas são importantes para definir parte do escopo e público alvo do seu evento, e como ele poderá ser organizado.

    4. Definindo os conteúdos

    Defina os conteúdos que terão no evento, isso pode ser feito abrindo um C4P (Call For Papers, ou Chamada para palestrantes no português adaptado), e avaliando as palestras e conteúdos enviados, sempre buscando a diversidade, colocando pessoas de minorias sociais no seu evento em posições de apresentação. Isso é algo muito valioso.

    Uma outra forma é convidando pessoas específicas para o seu evento. Essa forma é interessante principalmente para convidar pessoas renomadas naquela área para um keynote (a palestra principal de um evento), ou pessoas que você ou o resto da equipe do evento já conhecem, sabendo o que aquela pessoa pode apresentar no seu evento.

    5. Como será organizado?

    Nessa etapa, deve ser discutido como o evento será organizado e montado. Algumas perguntas são:

    • Terá uma pessoa host guiando o evento?
    • Terão palestras ocorrendo simultaneamente ou apenas de forma síncrona?
    • Como será a ordem das palestras?
    • Terá uma roda de conversa após o evento (painel)?
    • Terá algum tipo de happyhour depois com as pessoas participantes?

    Essas perguntas são muito importantes para a divulgação do evento, com isso as pessoas participantes poderão se guiar e se planejar para seu evento.

    6. Como e onde será transmitido?

    É importante definir como e onde esse evento será transmitido, algumas perguntas para a definição disso são:

    • Em qual plataforma o evento será transmitido?
    • A plataforma exige login ou não?
    • Vai ser em uma plataforma pública (Youtube, Twitch,etc.) ou privada? (Zoom, Meet,etc.)

    Pessoalmente, eu prefiro em plataformas públicas, e sem login necessário, como a Twitch, mas é uma decisão que varia de evento pra evento, e se o evento é pago ou gratuito.

    7. Divulgação

    Após a maior parte das outras etapas estarem definidas, a divulgação do evento deve começar.

    Eu recomendo que o início da divulgação seja 20 dias antes do começo do evento, com no total 6 posts de divulgação. Para exemplo, se o evento será dia 20, e terá 5 palestras com um painel no final, o calendário seria esse:

    01 - 1º Post divulgando o evento de forma geral, apresentando o evento e as palestras, mas sem aprofundar muito. 05 - 2º Post divulgando o evento de forma geral, apresentando o evento e as palestras, mas sem aprofundar muito, mas com outras imagens e textos. 10 - Post divulgando a 1º palestra 11 - Post divulgando a 2º palestra 12 - Post divulgando a 3º palestra 13 - Post divulgando a 4º palestra 14 - Post divulgando a 5º palestra 14 - Post divulgando o painel 19 - Post divulgando o evento novamente, deixando claro que o evento é amanhã

    20 - Post divulgando o evento no dia.

    8. Dia do evento

    O grande dia chegou! Avise as pessoas palestrantes, painelistas, hosts,etc. do evento, e só bora!

    Erros e problemas técnicos vão acontecer durante o evento, e tá tudo bem. Errar é algo inerente de qualquer evento, e até mesmo o tornam mais humanos. Então apenas faça graça quando algum problema acontecer, resolva-o e continue.

    Finalização

    Nesse artigo você aprendeu os passos para organizar um evento remoto, desde a ideia até o dia do evento.

    Obrigada por ler ❤️

    𝕁𝕦𝕝𝕚𝕒 👩🏻‍💻 - Aug 7

    Waleedh Nihal Ahmed - Aug 7

item.title: Odin 项目更新：第一部分 | The Odin Project Update: Part I
item.description:
尚泰 P.

发表于我完成课程的经历非常好。 我花了大约 8 周的时间，因为我并没有完全从头开始学习。 我已经熟悉 HTML、CSS 和使用文档对象模型。

我只是希望我早点知道这门课程。 我其实很喜欢 TOP 强迫你像真正的程序员一样学习和思考的方式。 在我与 FreeCodeCamp 一起学习的所有 Udemy 课程中，我完成的项目确实具有挑战性，但方式很好。 我了解了更多关于如何使用 JavaScript 构建项目的信息。 它还帮助我学习如何解决问题。

尽管在我对使用 JavaScript 更有信心之前我还有很长的路要走，但我想说这门课程对我帮助很大。 是的，这涉及到很多阅读，但如果它是我已经知道的，我通常会略读。 但总的来说，我通常会花时间慢慢处理事情，以确保我理解一切。 课程最困难的部分开始于使用真正的 JavaScript 代码来构建项目的时候。 真正的，我的意思是超过 10 行 JavaScript 代码。

学习这门课程让我走出了自己的舒适区，被迫像真正的程序员一样思考和编码。 现在，我更兴奋地看到全栈 JavaScript 课程将提供什么。 我很确定这些项目会更具挑战性，但是，只要我在学习和培养更好的解决问题的能力，我就全力以赴！

以下是我迄今为止构建的项目：

原文:

Chantae P.

Posted on Aug 7

My experience completing the curriculum has been really good. It took me about 8 weeks since I wasn't exactly learning from scratch. I was already familiar with HTML, CSS and using the Document Object Model.

I just wished I had known about this course sooner. I actually like the way TOP forces you to learn and think like a real programmer. Of all the Udemy courses I've taken along with FreeCodeCamp, the projects I've completed have been really challenging but in a good way. I learned more about how to build projects using JavaScript. And it also helped me learn how to problem solve.

Although I still have a long way to go until I feel more confident using JavaScript, I would say this course has helped me a ton. Yeah, it's a lot of reading involved, but usually I skim through it if it's something I already know. But overall, I usually take my time and process things slowly to make sure I understand everything. The most difficult portion of the course begins when it's time to use REAL JavaScript code to build the project. And by REAL, I mean more than 10 lines of JavaScript code.

Taking this course has pushed me out of my comfort zone and forced to to think and code like a real programmer. Now, I'm even more excited to see what the Full Stack JavaScript curriculum will have in store. I'm pretty sure the projects will be even more challenging but hey, as long as I'm learning and developing better problem solving skills, I'm all for it!

item.title: Java Efetivo - | Java Efetivo -
item.description:
保拉·桑塔纳

发表于手抄报...

细绳

//exemplo A // Neste exemplo o Java irá verificar se já existe uma instância na memória com a mesma sequência de caracteres String nomeA = "Paula" ; //exemplo B //Neste exemplo será criada uma nova instância em memória apesar de já termos uma instância com a mesma sequência de caracteres String nomeB = new String ( "Paula" );

科莫？

主要情况

自动装箱

// O código abaixo terá um problema de desempenho pois toda vez que a variável i que é primitiva for somada a variável soma que é um tipo primitivo empacotado, ou seja uma classe,é criada uma nova instância. Integer total = 1 ; for ( int i = 0 ; i < 100 ; i ++) { total += i ; } // O livro deixa claro que devemos sempre preferir o uso de primitivos e evitarmos o uso de autoboxing involuntário no nosso código.

生命之家

分析 quando usar

雅库布·安德杰耶夫斯基—— 一旦暂停，psanrosa13 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，psanrosa13 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，psanrosa13 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 psanrosa13 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 psanrosa13 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 psanrosa13 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Paula Santana

Posted on Aug 7

Esta é uma série baseada no entendimento de tópicos relacionados ao livro com foco no resumo.

Iniciando os trabalhos...

O livro cita a importância de reutilização de objetos, especialmente quando um novo objeto tem a mesma função. Além disso ele ressalta a importância da imutabilidade de objetos, pois isso facilita mais ainda a reutilização.

E para tomar como exemplo ele usa a classe String, que além de ser imutável é um ótimo exemplo de reutilização do nosso dia a dia.

String

//exemplo A
// Neste exemplo o Java irá verificar se já existe uma instância na memória com a mesma sequência de caracteres
String nomeA = "Paula"; //exemplo B
//Neste exemplo será criada uma nova instância em memória apesar de já termos uma instância com a mesma sequência de caracteres
String nomeB = new String("Paula");

Como?

Uma das formas de evitarmos a criação desnecessária de objetos seria através do uso de métodos static factory, no lugar de construtores em classes imutáveis. Apesar de podermos fazer isso em classes mutáveis precisamos considerar que elas podem sofrer alterações ao longo de sua utilização então poderia perder o propósito de ser reutilizada.

Se o objeto requer muito trabalho para ser criado vale considerarmos armazená-lo em cache para facilitar seu uso.

Mais situações

O livro cita exemplos internos do Java que utilizam essa premissa e outras partes que foram recriados e utilizaram para melhorar sua performance.
Mas diria que outro ponto que é citado que acamos utilizando muito no dia a dia é o uso de autoboxing .
Autoboxing e Autounboxing é o uso de primitivos com tipos primitivos empacotados.
Vamos ver um exemplo a seguir.

Autoboxing

// O código abaixo terá um problema de desempenho pois toda vez que a variável i que é primitiva for somada a variável soma que é um tipo primitivo empacotado, ou seja uma classe,é criada uma nova instância.  Integer total = 1;
for (int i = 0; i < 100; i++) { total += i;
} // O livro deixa claro que devemos sempre preferir o uso de primitivos e evitarmos o uso de autoboxing involuntário no nosso código.

Casos para evitar

O uso dessa abordagem em alguns cenários deve ser avaliada, isso se aplica apenas em algumas situações, em especial construção de objetos complexos, como por exemplo em casos em pool de objetos. O livro até cita como exemplo o pool de conexão com banco de dados, que no caso o custo de se obter esse objeto vale a sua reutilização, mas nem sempre compensa reutilizarmos, devemos analisar situação a situação.

Analise quando usar

Outro ponto abordado é que esse item do livro vai de encontro com outro item que incentiva cópias defensivas, e ele explica que nesse cenário é importante ter em mente que cópias defensivas evitam problemas através de bugs e falhas de segurança, enquanto a reutilização de instâncias tem como objetivo estilo e desempenho.O que faria o item de cópias defensivas ser mais importante do que evitar a criação de objetos.

Jakub Andrzejewski - Aug 1

item.title: 详细配置文件 | Provision Profile in details
item.description:
在上一篇文章中，我们介绍了代码签名的概念并回答了一些问题：我们为什么需要它以及它有什么好处。 我们甚至创建了一个带有上传密钥的签名 APK 来巩固知识。

正如我之前提到的，在 iOS 上进行代码签名的想法保持不变，但工作方式不同。 作为开发人员，我们仍然需要签署应用程序以使其免受恶意操作，但它通过证书和配置文件执行，而不是像我们在 Android 上那样手动执行。

但为什么？

为什么我们需要配置文件

如您所知，在 Android 上，我们可以轻松构建 APK 文件并直接在手机上下载此应用程序。 操作系统可能会显示一些应用程序不安全的警告，然后强制允许从未知来源安装，但粗略地说，Android 并不禁止我们安装未知应用程序（在商店外）并在平台上使用它。

同时，iOS 以更严格的方式处理这个过程。 即使是为了测试，我们也无法下载应用程序文件并直接安装它。 这就是弹出配置文件的地方。

基本上，此配置文件是一种数字指令，将开发人员和其他团队成员，或者更确切地说，他们的设备与 iPhone 开发团队联系起来，使他们能够下载和运行应用程序以进行测试。 如果预期帐户不在 Provision 配置文件中，它将无法运行该应用程序。

配置文件的组成部分

为了创建一个 Provision 配置文件，我们需要了解它的组成。 通常，它包含三个组件。 让我们从最简单的列表开始，即列出唯一设备和 App ID。

  • 设备列表是我们想要在其上运行应用程序的真实设备的唯一标识符。 这些标识符是我们可以从 iPhone 上的设置中获取的简单 UDID。

  • 它是包标识符的精确副本，我们可以使用 XCode 将其提取到项目文件夹中。 通常，它看起来像是网站 ( com.mycompany.myproject ) 的反向 URL。

第三个组成部分是证书。 它们之间的主要区别在于它们允许多少人下载和运行应用程序。 大多数情况下，我们使用开发和分发证书。

  • 开发证书只能用于测试，因为它包含我们的应用程序将运行的唯一设备列表。 如果设备的 UIDI 不在证书中，则应用程序将不会启动。 有了这个证书，我们就不能在 App Store 或 Testflight 上分发应用程序。

  • 分发证书不列出设备，因此任何设备都可以运行应用程序。 这也意味着，有了这个证书，就可以在 App Store 或 Testflight 上发布应用程序。

  • 当您需要测试不包含在您组织的 iOS 开发人员计划中的最多 100 人的应用程序时，Ad Hoc 证书也用于开发过程。

  • 当我们想要在更大的测试人员组（超过 100 人）中测试应用程序时，企业证书可以帮助我们。 当我们不想收集每个测试人员的设备 UDID 时，这是一个很好的解决方案。

您可以在您的配置文件中持有一个或多个证书。

现在我们可以自信地说，Provision Profile 通常包含三个组件：证书列表、设备列表、App ID。 当您在真实的 iOS 设备上运行应用程序时，它会检查这些组件以确定配置文件是否对该特定设备有效。 基于一个结论，应用程序运行或失败。

在开发和使用模拟器期间，Provisioning 配置文件也在幕后自动创建。 我们甚至没有考虑这一点，但 XCode 会处理这个问题。

了解所有细节后，我们终于可以进入 Provision 配置文件的加密过程。

钥匙串和企业社会责任

每个 macOS 都有一个名为 Keychain Access 的内置应用程序。 它存储您的密码和帐户信息，并减少您必须记住的密码数量。 但它也有助于创建和管理证书。 正是我们正在寻找的。

通过钥匙串，我们可以向苹果证书颁发机构请求初始证书。 该证书称为 CSR（证书签名请求），其中包含我们的个人信息以及从本地计算机生成的公钥。 我们用于解密的私钥保留在我们的钥匙串中。

稍后，当我们尝试为 Provision 配置文件（例如 Development、Distribution 等）创建证书时，Apple 将要求我们提供标识。 通过适当的 CSR，Apple 将我们识别为应用程序的开发人员，并允许我们下载必须保存到钥匙串中的 Provision 证书。

回顾

配置文件对代码进行数字签名，并确认它是由预期的实体制作的，并且在签名后没有被修改。 这为最终用户提供了信任，并为整个平台提供了安全性。

来源

  • 西奥多


原文:

In the previous article we covered the code signing concept and answered questions: why we need this and what benefits it gives. We even created a signed APK with an upload key to consolidate the knowledge.

As I mentioned before, the idea of code signing on iOS remains the same but works differently. As developers we still need to sign the application to make it protected against malicious actions, but it performs through certificates and a Provision profile, not manually as we did it on Android.

But why?

Why we need a Provision profile

As you know, on Android we can easily build an APK file and directly download this application on a phone. The operating system may show some warnings that the app isn't secured, then enforce to allow installation from unknown sources but roughly speaking Android doesn't prohibit us from installing an unknown application (outside the store) and using it on the platform.

Meanwhile, iOS approaches this process in a more strict way. Even for testing we cannot download an application file and directly install it. Apple cares about security of the platform and doesn't allow it to install third-party applications that haven't passed through an Apple signing process. And that's where a Provisioning profile pops up.

Basically, this profile is a digital instruction that ties developers and other team members, or rather say their devices, to an iPhone Development Team and enable them to download and run the application for testing purposes. If an expecting account isn't in the Provision profile it won't be able to run the application.

Components of Provision profile

In order to create a Provision profile we need to understand what it consists of. Usually, it has three components. Let's start from the easiest ones which are List unique devices and App ID.

  • List of devices is unique identifiers of the real devices that we want to run our application on. These identifiers are simple UDIDs that we can take from settings on an IPhone.

  • App ID is a string that identifies the application to Apple. It's an exact copy of a bundle identifier that we can extract with XCode in a project's folder. Usually, it looks like a reversed URL to a website (com.mycompany.myproject).

The third component is a certificate. Apple has various types of certificates and all of them are intended for different development stages. The main difference between them is how many people they allow to download and run an application. Mostly we use Development and Distribution certificates.

  • Development certificate can be used only for testing because it includes a list of unique devices that our application is going to run on. If devices' UIDI isn't in the certificate then an application won't launch. With this certificate we cannot distribute an application on App Store or Testflight.

  • Distribution certificate doesn't list devices thus any device can run an application. It also means, with this certificate it's possible to publish an application on App Store or Testflight.

  • Ad Hoc certificate is also used in the development process when you need to test an application up to 100 people that are not included in the iOS developer program for your organization.

  • Enterprise certificate helps us when we want to test an application in a larger group of testers (above 100). It's a great solution when we don't want to collect every single testers' device UDID.

You can hold one or more certificates in your Provision profile.

Now we can confidently say that Provision profile usually contains three components: list of certificates, list of devices, App ID. When you run an application on a real iOS device it checks these components to determine whether the Profile is valid for this particular device or not. Based on a conclusion the application runs or fails.

During a development and working with a simulator the Provisioning profile is also being created automatically behind the scenes. We don't even think about that but XCode takes care of this.

Knowing all the particulars we finally can step into the encryption process of Provision profile.

Keychain and CSR

Every macOS has a built-in application which is called Keychain Access. It stores your passwords and account information, and reduces the number of passwords you have to remember. But it also helps to create and manage certificates. Exactly what we are looking for.

Through Keychain we can request an initial certificate from Certificate Authority which is Apple. This certificate is called CSR (Certificate Signing Request) and has our personal information alongside a generated public key from the local machine. The private key that we use for decryption stays in our Keychain.

CSR works as a digital signature that we've talked about in the previous article. Later on, when we try to create a certificate for Provision profile (such as Development, Distribution, etc), Apple will request from us an identification. And CSR will work as this identification because that contains our public key. With the proper CSR, Apple identifies us as a developer of the application and allow us to download a Provision certificate which we must save to our Keychain.

Recap

Provision profile digitally signs the code and confirms that it was made by an expected entity and hasn't been modified after the signing. This provides trust for the end-user and security for the whole platform.

Sources

  • iCode
  • Apple Documentation
  • Infinum Handbook
  • Theodo
item.author: aynurkarimov

item.title: 在开源项目中写好票 | Writing great tickets in open source projects
item.description:
在过去的几个月里，我一直在做一个“新的”开源项目——一个开源企业 Stripe App ！
在编写问题/工单时如何改进协作？ 阅读更多以了解我的想法

在开源项目上写好问题单的重要性

如果您正在与分布在互联网上的其他人一起开发一个开源项目（在 github 上），那么编写清晰简洁的问题单很重要。

这样，每个人都知道需要做什么，没有人会浪费时间做不必要的事情。

有几种不同的方法可以编写问题单。

第一个：使用模板。

开源项目的问题模板

您可以在线或在各种文本编辑器（如 VS Code）中找到大量 github 问题模板。 使用模板可确保您在工单中包含所有✨重要信息。✨

但是，模板可能有点乏味。 您可能必须自定义模板以匹配您正在处理的项目类型，因此请明智地选择。

编写问题单的另一种方法是简单地包含问题的标题和简要描述。 这通常足以解决小问题。 但是，对于较大的问题，包含更多信息（例如屏幕截图、重现问题的步骤等）通常会有所帮助。

一般来说，最好是在信息过多而不是过少方面犯错。 这样，分配任务的任何人都可以快速了解问题并开始修复。

如何在开源项目上写好问题单

有一些最佳实践可供遵循。 首先，一定要包含清晰简洁的标题和描述。

如果您的问题与特定代码段有关，请将其也包含在票证中。 包括以下内容也很有帮助：

  • 日志
  • 截图
  • 最后，请务必标记问题（不要直接标记项目成员），以便您可以对问题进行适当的分类。

    写好问题的技巧

    如果您不熟悉开源、协作，或者只是想提高您的问题报告技能，请继续阅读有关如何编写好的问题单的一些提示。

    这将帮助其他人快速了解问题所在以及他们是否可以提供帮助。

    包括屏幕截图或截屏视频，如果它们有助于理解问题。
    它可能有助于以某种形式或其他形式塑造解决方案 - 顺便说一句，这完全是可选的！

    希望这会有所帮助，我一直在将这种方法用于我自己在 Github 上的工作，并且我认为它的效果很好！



原文:

In the last couple of months, I have been working on a "new" open source project - an open source enterprise Stripe App!
How can I improve collaboration when writing issues/tickets? Read more to learn my thoughts

The importance of writing good issue tickets on open source projects

If you're working on an open source project (on github) with other people spread across the internet, it's important to write clear and concise issue tickets.

This way, everyone knows what needs to be done and no one wastes time doing things that aren't necessary.

There are a few different ways to go about writing an issue ticket.

The first one: use a template.

Issue templates for open source projects

You can find heaps of github issue templates online or in various text editors (like VS Code). Using a template ensures that you include all ✨ the important information in your ticket.✨

However, templates can be a little dull. You might have to customize templates to match the type of project you're working on, so choose wisely.

Another way to write an issue ticket is to simply include a title and brief description of the problem. This is usually sufficient for small issues. However, for larger issues, it's often helpful to include more information such as screenshots, steps to reproduce the problem, etc.

In general, it's best to err on the side of too much information rather than too little. That way, whoever is assigned the task can quickly understand the problem and get started on a fix.

How to write a good issue ticket on an open source project

There are a few best practices to follow. First, be sure to include clear and concise titles and descriptions.

If your issue is related to a specific piece of code, include that in the ticket as well. It's also helpful to include:

  • logs
  • screenshots
  • .. or videos if applicable.

Finally, be sure to tag the issue (do NOT tag the members of the project directly) so you can categorize appropriately the issue.

Tips for writing a good issue

If you're new to open source, collaboration, or just want to improve your issue-reporting skills, read on for some tips on how to write a good issue ticket.

1️⃣ First, be sure to include a clear and concise summary of the issue in the title. This will help others quickly understand what the problem is and whether they can help.

2️⃣ Next, provide as much detail as possible in the body of the issue. Include screenshots or screencasts if they would be helpful in understanding the problem.
(Speaking of screencasts - my friend Ian is working on making screencasts awesome at replayable.io if you want to give him some love - I would be appreciative of your time)

👉 And be sure to include any relevant error messages or logs.

3️⃣ Finally, if you have any ideas about how to fix the issue, feel free to include them in the issue ticket. It might help shape the solution, in a form or another - by the way, this is totally optional!

Hope this helps, I've been using this method for my own work on Github and I think it has been working out pretty well!
item.author: orliesaurus

item.title: 如何让招聘流程“更人性化” | How to make the Recruitment process "more humane"
item.description:
招聘过程可以改变候选人甚至公司的生活。 毕竟，一个好的团队决定了任何一家公司的命运，每个员工都为此做出了贡献。 不幸的是，很多时候招聘人员和决策者最终将选拔过程变成了可怕的考验。 我并不是说每个人都是故意这样做的，但是，科技行业的很大一部分似乎都是这种情况。 这篇文章只是我试图反弹一些我认为需要被招聘行业听到的想法，以帮助更多符合条件的人找到好工作。

一个人如何以及在何处学习他们的技能不是一个因素

您的团队需要一个可以完成任务的人，如果候选人有能力这样做，那么他们就足够熟练了。 拥有正规教育（或大学学位）并不需要被认为比自学成才的候选人更好或更差。

自学成才的人往往会表现出神奇的决心、自我激励、克服逆境和掌握概念的能力。 但是，让我明确一点，我并不是说正规教育更容易或表现出更少的素质。

我的观点是，这不应该是一个与另一个的问题。 它应该只是对候选人能力的公正评价。

将候选人视为“人”

尽快通知他们拒绝和接受。 不要让不确定性迫在眉睫或给予虚假的希望。 快速拒绝通常比没有回应更痛苦。 您可能造成的影响可能是无法估量的。 这不仅仅是一份工作，更是“生活”的问题。 候选人需要为自己和亲人的生活提前计划。

孩子们可能需要新的 apt 学校。 配偶可能需要管理他们的时间安排。 有家庭成员需要医疗帮助的人需要做出安排。 住宿、旅游、设施等需要管理。 生活的许多方面不必要地停顿或漂浮在不确定中。

你越早给候选人你的决定，他们就能越早变得更加清晰，并开始他们的下一步生活。

更好的拒绝

除了让他们快速，如果将导致拒绝的具体标准传达给候选人也是有用的。 这可以帮助他们在这些方面提高自己，从而增加他们在下一次面试中成功的机会。

这似乎是一件苦差事，但如果您决定拒绝候选人，您已经充分意识到角色和候选人之间存在的缺点或错位。 花几分钟时间来传达这些信息对候选人来说是非常有益的，并且还可以让你的公司享有不将员工视为机械且易于更换的替身的声誉。

了解角色的实际要求

广撒网以邀请所有可能的技术/技能的人是浪费您的时间和他们的努力。 此外，它只会增加公司的成本，同时大大增加您的团队需要筛选的不相关应用程序的数量。

仔细制定一组与特定角色实际相关的要求将对所有相关人员都有帮助。 不必要地在空缺广告中包含所有可能的条款不仅会使一些优秀的候选人望而却步，而且还可能导致您雇用可能不是最合适的人。

包括有关所使用的相关技术的信息、该角色的职责以及预期的软技能，可以帮助吸引那些为工作做好准备的人。 这也将使应届生和自学成才的候选人更好地评估自己，并知道他们有机会申请。

逼真的技能匹配

大多数角色不需要 100% 了解技术或语言的各个方面。 你真正需要的是具备足够的基础知识、愿意学习、有解决问题的能力以及对他人有正确态度的候选人。 然后你就有了一个可靠且面向未来的团队成员。

让条款明确说明哪些概念对于日常履行该职位是强制性的，然后提及其他有助于在该职位上表现出色的要求，可以帮助您的公司和潜在申请人更好地了解该职位。

将候选人与处理大部分角色所需的实际概念相匹配可能就足够了。 其余的可以由优秀的候选人在工作中学习。

拒绝候选人而不考虑他们有提升技能的潜力对任何一方都没有帮助。

薪酬正义

工作是一种收入手段……是实现财务自由的一种方式。 无可否认。 因此，无论一个角色有多充实或有用，员工都需要一份与所需努力和所需技能相匹配的薪酬。 此外，它需要是一种不基于性别、种族、国籍等，而是基于技能的薪酬。 员工不仅在处理您的任务，而且还为工作贡献了他们“生活”的每一天。 他们应该得到相应的补偿。 所有候选人，都是值得同等尊重的人

一个勤劳的人因为人们的偏见或偏见而被剥夺了体面的生活，是全人类的污点。

经验大于经验

对于大多数工作来说，需要的是解决可能出现的问题的能力，或者使用一些基本技术/技能创造新事物的能力。 一个尝试过 100 个项目或在多个项目上花费大量时间和精力的新手可能拥有足够（甚至更多）的技能，作为一个工作多年的人。 这对于实际的人和工作来说自然是主观的，但是仅仅拥有多年的经验不应该是考虑的主要因素。

有时候选人可能精通处理实际工作，但可能不知道他们正在做什么的相关“技术术语”。 除非工作绝对有必要（例如，在担任教学角色的情况下），*不知道确切的术语不一定是衡量候选人技能的标准。 结论

这一切都不是指责或使其成为美国对他们。 目的是表明我们实际上都是同一个生态系统的一部分。 今天的招聘人员可能需要成为明天的候选人。 今天的候选人可能拥有一家公司或明天成为决策者。 因此，采取正确的步骤有助于强化人们付出足够努力就能找到工作，而无需依赖运气或招聘人员的心情。

一个强大的“过程”到位当然是必要的，但是，不能忘记人的因素。 心态的轻微改变，以及评估人们做出的小决定的后果的意愿，可能会对每个人产生巨大而直接的影响。

此外，让每个人都更人性化的事情有助于整个行业（更不用说，无论如何这是正确的做法）。 我们需要提醒自己，我们属于同一个“社会动物”群体（人类），需要相互支持才能生存和发展。 我们不应该屈服于成为彼此恐惧或享受他人痛苦的掠食者和猎物。

这显然不是一些研究驱动的文章，可能与它完全相反。 所以，我相信我上面提到的观点会有很多分歧。 随时在评论中添加它们，以便我们互相学习。 关键是要对这一切进行健康的讨论，这样我们就可以让彼此意识到并朝着正确的方向前进……一起。



原文:

The recruitment process can be life-altering for the candidate, and even for the company. A good team is after all what decides the fate of any company, and every employee contributes towards it. Unfortunately, many times the recruiters and the decision-makers end up turning the selection process into a dreaded ordeal. I'm not saying that everyone does so on purpose, but, it seems to be the case in a huge chunk of the tech industry. This post is just my attempt at bouncing off some ideas that i think need to be heard by the recruitment industry, to help more eligible people to get good jobs.

How & Where a person learned their skills is not a factor

Your team needs a person that can complete the task, if the candidate is capable of doing so, then they are skilled enough. Having a formal education (or college degree) doesn't need to be considered better or worse than a self-taught candidate.

A self-taught person would have often shown miraculous determination, ability to self-motivate, overcome adversities and grasp concepts by themselves. But, let me be clear, i'm not saying that a formal education is any easier or displays any less calibre.

My point is that it shouldn't be a matter of one vs. the other. It should just be an unbiased evaluation of the candidate's abilities.

Consider candidates as 'people'

Inform them about rejections & acceptance as soon as possible. Don't let uncertainty loom or give false hope. A quick rejection is often less painful than no response. The impact you may be causing could be immeasurable. It is not just a job, it is a matter of 'life'. The candidate needs to plan ahead for their own life, and for that of their loved ones.

Kids may need new apt schools. Spouses may need to manage their time schedules. People having family members that need medical-assistance would need to make arrangements. Accomodation,travel,facilities ,etc. need to be managed. So many facets of life unnecessarily paused or floating in uncertainty.

The sooner you give the candidate your decision, the sooner they can have more clarity and resume their next steps in life.

Better Rejections

Along with making them quick, it would also be useful if the specific criteria that caused the rejection are conveyed to the candidate. This can help them improve themselves in those areas, hence increasing their chances of succeeding in their next interview.

It may seem like a chore, but if you've decided to reject the candidate, you are already fully aware of the shortcomings or the mis-alignment that is present between the role and the candidate. Spending a few minutes to convey this information can be immensely beneficial to the candidate, and can also give your company a reputation for being someone that doesn't view employees as a mechanical and easily replaceable stand-in.

Understand the actual requirements for the role

Casting a broad net to invite people from all possible tech/skills is a waste of your time & their efforts. Also, it just adds more cost to the company, along with drastically increasing the amount of irrelevant applications that your team will need to sift through.

Carefully crafting a set of requirements that are actually relevant to the specific role would be helpful for all involved. Unnecessarily including all possible terms into the vacancy ad will not just keep away some good candidates, but it could also lead you to hiring someone that may not be the best fit.

Including information about the relevant tech used, responsibilities that will come under the role and the soft-skills that are expected, can help to attract people who are more prepared for the job. This would also make freshers and self-taught candidates to evaluate themselves better and know that they have a chance to apply.

Realistic skill matching

Most roles don't need 100% knowledge of every aspect of a tech or language. What you actually need to find are candidates with enough fundamental knowledge, willingness to learn, ability to solve problems & have the right attitude towards others. Then you have a reliable & future-proof team-member.

Making the terms clear about what concepts will be mandatory for the daily fulfillment of the role, and then mentioning other requirements which could help to excel in the role, could help to give your company and potential applicants, a better understanding of the role.

Matching the candidate with the actual concepts that are needed to handle a majority of the role is probably enough. The rest can be learned on the job by a good candidate.

Denying candidates without considering that they have the potential to upgrade their skills is not helpful for either party.

Justice In Pay

A job is a means for an income...a way towards financial freedom. There is no denying it. So regardless of how fulfilling or useful a role is, an employee needs a pay that is justified for the effort required and the skills needed. Also, it needs to be a pay not discriminated based on gender, race, nationality, etc but on skill. The employee is not just handling your tasks, but is also contributing a daily chunk of their 'life' towards the job. They deserve to be compensated accordingly. All candidates, are human-beings that deserve equal respect

A hard-working individual being denied a decent life due to the biases or prejudices of people , is a stain on all of humanity.

Experiences over experience

For most jobs, what is required is the ability to solve problems that may arise, or create new things with the use of some basic technology/skills. A newbie that has tried doing a 100 projects or spent a lot of time & efforts on multiple projects could have enough(maybe even more) skills as a person experienced for years at a job. It is naturally subjective to the actual person and the job, but having years of experience alone shouldn't be a prime factor that is considered.

Sometimes a candidate may be proficient in handling the actual work , but may not know the relevant 'technical term' for what they are doing. Unless it is absolutely necessary for the job ,(maybe for example in case of a teaching role) , *not knowing the exact terminology doesn't have to be a measure of the candidate's skills. *

CONCLUSION

None of this is to point fingers or make it an US vs. THEM. The aim is to suggest that we are all actually part of the same ecosystem. A recruiter today may need to be a candidate tomorrow. A candidate today may own a company or be the decision-maker tomorrow. So, taking the right steps can help to reinforce that people putting enough effort can get a job without needing to rely on luck or the mood of the recruiter.

A robust 'process' being in place is certainly a necessity, but, the human element cannot be forgotten. A slight change in mindset, and willingness to evaluate the ramifications of the small decisions that are taken by people, could probably have a large and immediate impact for everyone.

Also, making things more humane for everyone helps the industry as a whole (not to mention, it is the right thing to do anyway). We need to remind ourselves that we belong to the same 'social animal' group (humans) that need to support each other to survive, and thrive. We should not stoop down to being predators and preys that fear each other or relish in the suffering of another.

This was obviously not some research-driven article, and is probably the exact opposite of it. So, I'm sure there'll be many disagreements to the points I've mentioned above. Feel free to add them in the comments so that we can learn from each other. The whole point is to have a healthy discussion about all this so that we can make each other aware and move forward towards whatever is right..together.
item.author: notanothervivek

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 ztologame 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

ZtoloGame

Posted on Aug 7

To turn it off:

old_logger = ActiveRecord::Base.logger ActiveRecord::Base.logger = nil

To turn it back on:

ActiveRecord::Base.logger = old_logger
Solution 2:

Here's a variation I consider somewhat cleaner, that still allows potential other logging from AR. In config/environments/development.rb :

config.after_initialize do ActiveRecord::Base.logger = Rails.logger.clone ActiveRecord::Base.logger.level = Logger::INFO end

Solution 3:

This might not be a suitable solution for the console, but Rails has a method for this problem: Logger#silence

ActiveRecord::Base.logger.silence do # the stuff you want to be silenced

end

Waleedh Nihal Ahmed - Aug 7

item.title: 如何创建你的第一个 npm 包？ 🧰 🧰 | How to create your first npm package? 🧰
item.description:
理查德·多布罗

发表于包含超过 200 万个 JavaScript 包的在线存储库。

没有帐户？

导航到npmjs.com并单击注册。

创建一个名为arraySort的文件夹

这个过程会自动生成一个带有默认选项的package.json文件。

在这个过程中，你会被问到一些问题，比如作者的名字、描述和许可证等。

您应该提供存储库及其功能的简要说明。

你做到了！

恭喜，你已经发布了你的第一个 NPM 包！

一旦暂停，dobron 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，dobron 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，dobron 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 dobron 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 dobron 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 dobron 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Richard Dobroň

Posted on Aug 7

NPM stands for Node Package Manager and is the default JavaScript package manager powered by Node.js. The npm manager has two parts:

  • CLI (Command Line Interface) — a tool for publishing and downloading packages,
  • online repositories containing over 2 million JavaScript packages.

Don't have an account?

Navigate to npmjs.com and click sign up.

1. Create an npm project

  • Create a folder named arraySort

2. Initialize project as npm package

a) Using npm init -y or -yes

  • This process will automatically generate a package.json file with default options.

b) Using npm init

  • In this process, you will be asked a number of questions such as the name of the author, the description, and the license, etc.

3. Create a file named index.js with this content: 

function arraySort(array, compareFunction) {
    const cloned = array.slice();
    if (compareFunction) {
        return cloned.sort(compareFunction);
    }

    return cloned.sort();
}

module.exports = arraySort;

4. Create a README.md (optional)

  • You should provide a brief description of the repository and its functions.

5. Login into NPM

6. Publish the package

You Did It!

Congratulations, you have published your first NPM package!

item.title: M1 macのc++でGLFWを使ってみる | M1 macのc++でGLFWを使ってみる
item.description:
环境

> uname -m arm64 > brew config macOS: 12.5-arm64 Rosetta 2: false

GLFWをインストール

> brew install glfw > brew --prefix glfw /opt/homebrew/opt/glfw

コンパイル・実行してみるコード

インストールしただけだとエラーになる

❯ g++ -o main main.cpp -lglfw -framework Cocoa -framework OpenGL -framework IOKit -framework CoreVideo main.cpp:1:10: fatal error: 'GLFW/glfw3.h' file not found #include <GLFW/glfw3.h>

CPATH, LIBRARY_PATHを环境変数に追加

> vim ~/.zshrc # 下記を追加export CPATH = /opt/homebrew/include export LIBRARY_PATH = /opt/homebrew/lib

コンパイル・実行成功

  • コンパイル成功したけど、警告が沢山出る。これはAppleがOpenGLを基本廃止（推非奨）にしているかららしい。
 ❯ g++ -o main main.cpp -lglfw -framework Cocoa -framework OpenGL -framework IOKit -framework CoreVideo main.cpp:23:5: warning: 'glClear' is deprecated: first deprecated in macOS 10.14 - OpenGL API deprecated. ( Define GL_SILENCE_DEPRECATION to silence these warnings ) [ -Wdeprecated-declarations ] glClear ( GL_COLOR_BUFFER_BIT ) ; ^ /Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/SDKs/MacOSX.sdk/System/Library/Frameworks/OpenGL.framework/Headers/gl.h:2394:13: note: 'glClear' has been explicitly marked deprecated here extern void glClear ( GLbitfield mask ) OPENGL_DEPRECATED ( 10.0, 10.14 ) ;

警告を非表示にしてみる

  • コードに下记をする。
    • Makefileを作る
      CPPFLAGS := -I src/includes FRAMEWORKS := -framework Cocoa -framework OpenGL -framework IOKit -framework CoreVideo LIBS := -lglfw BUILD_DIR := ./bin SRC_DIRS := ./src SRCS := $( shell find $(SRC_DIRS) -name * .cpp ) OBJS := $( addprefix $(BUILD_DIR) /, $( subst .cpp,.o, $( notdir $(SRCS) ))) $(BUILD_DIR)/main : $(OBJS) clang++ -o $@ $(LIBS) $(FRAMEWORKS) $^ $(BUILD_DIR)/%.o : src/%.cpp clang++ -c -o $@ $(CPPFLAGS) $< do : @ bin/main clean : rm -rf bin mkdir bin

      调试できない

      • vimspectorで、 .vimspector.jsonを下記のように設定してみたけど、エラーになる。 vimspectorで、 .vimspector.jsonを下记のように设定してみたけど、エラーになる。
      • -lglfwがあるとエラーになるっぽい。 -lglfwがあるとエラーになるっぽい。
       { "configurations" : { "CodeLLDB" : { "adapter" : "CodeLLDB" , "variables" : { "BUILDME" : { "shell" : "clang++ -o ${workspaceRoot}/bin/debug -g -lglfw -framework Cocoa -framework OpenGL -framework IOKit -framework CoreVideo ${workspaceRoot}/src/main.cpp" } }, "configuration" : { "request" : "launch" , "program" : "${workspaceRoot}/bin/debug" , "stdio" : [ null , null , null ] } } }
}

      青いウインドウを表示するコード

      #ifdef __APPLE__ #define GL_SILENCE_DEPRECATION #include <GLFW/glfw3.h> #endif #include <iostream>
using namespace std ; static void key_callback ( GLFWwindow * window , int key , int scancode , int action , int mods ) { if ( key == GLFW_KEY_ESCAPE && action == GLFW_PRESS ) glfwSetWindowShouldClose ( window , GLFW_TRUE ); } int main ( void ) { GLFWwindow * window ; if ( ! glfwInit ()) return - 1 ; glfwWindowHint ( GLFW_CONTEXT_VERSION_MAJOR , 3 ); glfwWindowHint ( GLFW_CONTEXT_VERSION_MINOR , 2 ); glfwWindowHint ( GLFW_OPENGL_FORWARD_COMPAT , GL_TRUE ); glfwWindowHint ( GLFW_OPENGL_PROFILE , GLFW_OPENGL_CORE_PROFILE ); window = glfwCreateWindow ( 640 , 480 , "Hello World" , NULL , NULL ); if ( ! window ) { cout << "failed to create window." << endl ; glfwTerminate (); return - 1 ; } cout << "opened window" << endl ; glfwSetKeyCallback ( window , key_callback ); glfwMakeContextCurrent ( window ); glfwSwapInterval ( 1 ); const GLubyte * renderer = glGetString ( GL_RENDERER ); const GLubyte * version = glGetString ( GL_VERSION ); std :: cout << "Renderer: " << renderer << std :: endl ; std :: cout << "OpenGL version supported: " << version << std :: endl ; glClearColor ( 0.0 f , 0.3 f , 0.6 f , 0.3 f ); while ( ! glfwWindowShouldClose ( window )) { glClear ( GL_COLOR_BUFFER_BIT ); glfwSwapBuffers ( window ); glfwPollEvents (); } glfwTerminate (); cout << "closed window" << endl ; }

      リポジトリ

      表示されるウインドウ

卡琼坎贝尔 - 艾琳谢弗 - 一旦暂停，web3ten0 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，web3ten0 将能够再次评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，web3ten0 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 web3ten0 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 web3ten0 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 web3ten0 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

OpenGLについてまだ全然分かっていませんが、GLFWを使ってアニメーションを表示したいと思っています。とりあえず、M1 macのc++でウインドウを表示しました。

環境 

> uname -m
arm64
> brew config
macOS: 12.5-arm64
Rosetta 2: false

GLFWをインストール 

> brew install glfw
> brew --prefix glfw
/opt/homebrew/opt/glfw

コンパイル・実行してみるコード

インストールしただけだとエラーになる 

❯ g++ -o main main.cpp -lglfw -framework Cocoa -framework OpenGL -framework IOKit -framework CoreVideo
main.cpp:1:10: fatal error: 'GLFW/glfw3.h' file not found
#include <GLFW/glfw3.h>

CPATH, LIBRARY_PATHを環境変数に追加 

> vim ~/.zshrc
# 下記を追加
export CPATH=/opt/homebrew/include
export LIBRARY_PATH=/opt/homebrew/lib

コンパイル・実行成功

  • コンパイル成功したけど、warningが沢山出る。これはAppleがOpenGLを基本廃止（非推奨）にしているかららしい。 
❯ g++ -o main main.cpp -lglfw -framework Cocoa -framework OpenGL -framework IOKit -framework CoreVideo
main.cpp:23:5: warning: 'glClear' is deprecated: first deprecated in macOS 10.14 - OpenGL API deprecated. (Define GL_SILENCE_DEPRECATION to silence these warnings) [-Wdeprecated-declarations] glClear(GL_COLOR_BUFFER_BIT); ^
/Library/Developer/CommandLineTools/SDKs/MacOSX.sdk/System/Library/Frameworks/OpenGL.framework/Headers/gl.h:2394:13: note: 'glClear' has been explicitly marked deprecated here
extern void glClear (GLbitfield mask) OPENGL_DEPRECATED(10.0, 10.14);

warningを非表示にしてみる

  • コードに下記を追加する。
    • #define GL_SILENCE_DEPRECATION によって、非表示になる。 
#ifdef __APPLE__
#define GL_SILENCE_DEPRECATION
#include <GLFW/glfw3.h>
#endif

Makefileを作る 

CPPFLAGS := -I src/includes
FRAMEWORKS := -framework Cocoa -framework OpenGL -framework IOKit -framework CoreVideo
LIBS := -lglfw BUILD_DIR := ./bin
SRC_DIRS := ./src
SRCS := $(shell find $(SRC_DIRS) -name *.cpp)
OBJS := $(addprefix $(BUILD_DIR)/, $(subst .cpp,.o, $(notdir $(SRCS)))) $(BUILD_DIR)/main: $(OBJS) clang++ -o $@ $(LIBS) $(FRAMEWORKS) $^ $(BUILD_DIR)/%.o: src/%.cpp clang++ -c -o $@ $(CPPFLAGS) $< do: @bin/main
clean: rm -rf bin mkdir bin

Debugできない

  • vimspectorで、.vimspector.jsonを下記のように設定してみたけど、エラーになる。
  • -lglfwがあるとエラーになるっぽい。 
{ "configurations": { "CodeLLDB": { "adapter": "CodeLLDB", "variables": { "BUILDME": { "shell": "clang++ -o ${workspaceRoot}/bin/debug -g -lglfw -framework Cocoa -framework OpenGL -framework IOKit -framework CoreVideo ${workspaceRoot}/src/main.cpp" } }, "configuration": { "request": "launch", "program": "${workspaceRoot}/bin/debug", "stdio": [null, null, null] } } }
}

青いウインドウを表示するコード 

#ifdef __APPLE__
#define GL_SILENCE_DEPRECATION
#include <GLFW/glfw3.h>
#endif

#include <iostream>
using namespace std; static void key_callback(GLFWwindow* window, int key, int scancode, int action, int mods) { if (key == GLFW_KEY_ESCAPE && action == GLFW_PRESS) glfwSetWindowShouldClose(window, GLFW_TRUE);
} int main(void) { GLFWwindow* window; if (!glfwInit()) return -1; glfwWindowHint(GLFW_CONTEXT_VERSION_MAJOR, 3); glfwWindowHint(GLFW_CONTEXT_VERSION_MINOR, 2); glfwWindowHint(GLFW_OPENGL_FORWARD_COMPAT, GL_TRUE); glfwWindowHint(GLFW_OPENGL_PROFILE, GLFW_OPENGL_CORE_PROFILE); window = glfwCreateWindow(640, 480, "Hello World", NULL, NULL); if (!window) { cout << "failed to create window." << endl; glfwTerminate(); return -1; } cout << "opened window" << endl; glfwSetKeyCallback(window, key_callback); glfwMakeContextCurrent(window); glfwSwapInterval(1); const GLubyte* renderer = glGetString(GL_RENDERER); const GLubyte* version = glGetString(GL_VERSION); std::cout << "Renderer: " << renderer << std::endl; std::cout << "OpenGL version supported: " << version << std::endl; glClearColor(0.0f, 0.3f, 0.6f, 0.3f); while (!glfwWindowShouldClose(window)) { glClear(GL_COLOR_BUFFER_BIT); glfwSwapBuffers(window); glfwPollEvents(); } glfwTerminate(); cout << "closed window" << endl;
}

リポジトリ

https://github.com/web3ten0/cpp-glfw-1

表示されるウインドウ

Cajaun Campbell - Dec 12 '21

Erin Schaffer - Dec 10 '21

item.title: 带有 Nginx 入口的 KeyCloak | KeyCloak with Nginx Ingress
item.description:
你好，如果你来到这里，我想你也厌倦了在 Kubernetes (K8s) 中找到使用 Ingress(Nginx) 部署 KeyCloak 的解决方案，我遇到了一些不太公开的问题，所以我在这里确保你没有经历过我经历过的痛苦😅所以让我们开始吧。

赠品

第一步

选择您要使用的图表，有 2 个掌舵图表

比特纳米钥匙披风
以代码为中心的 KeyCloak

随意使用其中的任何一个，您可以使用谷歌搜索或单击上面提供的链接。 对于此示例，我们将使用 Bitnami KeyCloak，我个人认为使用此图表更容易部署。

第二步

所以我猜你也决定使用 Bitnami 图表，所以你需要注意的事情很少，否则部署将失败。

注意

确保您已通过传入 values.yaml 为外部数据库设置密码

externalDatabase.password

postgresql.auth.password

这 2 个字段应该具有相同的值，否则您将遇到 postgres 错误并且 pod 将进入crashback-loop
由于我们使用 Nginx 作为 Ingress-Controller 我们将启用入口

ingress.enabled

ingress.hostname

ingress.pathType

我希望您在 values.yaml 中找到这些值并覆盖它们，这是最重要的事情，因为我们在我们的案例中使用了 Application Load Balancer（我将很快附上如何做到这一点的链接。）
我已经以这样的方式配置它，在 ALB 之前，所有流量都在HTTPS中，如果您有相同的情况，则从那里在HTTP中
确保您已完成此更改。

proxy: edge

您还可以配置用户名及其密码，希望您能找到这些值。

现在您可以使用更新的值部署掌舵图并等待几秒钟，因为您需要一些时间为自己拿一个水瓶🍾。

第三步

确认它正在成功运行尝试使用 kube-proxy 命令将端口代理到您的本地系统并查看它是否正在运行，如果是，那么我们可以继续前进，如果不是🥺 请检查您所做的配置或随时询问在评论中。

第四步

如果您在设置入口时已完成此步骤，那很好，但如果没有，您就像我一样😊。

我们需要配置我们的 Nginx 入口控制器来传递标头，所以在挖掘了 5 天后我发现了这个，
我们需要创建一个包含以下数据的 configmap

kind: ConfigMap apiVersion: v1 metadata: name: <chart-name-with-which-deployed>-nginx-ingress-controller namespace: <namespace-in-which-deployed-nginx-ingress-controller> data: use-forwarded-headers: "true" forwarded-for-header: "X-Forwarded-For"

并确保名称正确，否则将无法正常工作，要验证它是否正常工作，请查看 pod 的日志

nginx-controller-nginx-ingress-controller

你会看到类似的东西

Found the configmap needed to reload backend, reload complete

不完全是这样，你就完成了

现在转到与 keycloak 关联的主机名，您将能够毫无问题地访问管理面板。

如果这些步骤未完成，让我们讨论错误

首先，如果您在升级 helm chart 时没有设置密码，您将失去与 postgres 的连接，因为默认密码是随机生成的，它将在升级后更改，因此请确保您提供了密码。

其次，如果标题没有通过 Ingress 你将无法访问管理控制台，而不是你会被困在

/admin/master/console

如果它已经配置，您将不会遇到此错误。

第三个太多重定向

这是由于proxy=passthrough导致了这个错误。
及其默认值，因此请确保您的 tls 是否在 keycloak 前面的负载均衡器或代理处终止，那么您必须使用

proxy: edge

它会开始工作

和

我的朋友，如果您做对了所有这些，您将能够看到管理控制台的登录屏幕

随时问任何问题



原文:

Hello there, If you came here I guess you are also tired of finding the solution to Deploy KeyCloak with Ingress(Nginx) in Kubernetes (K8s), I have faced the some issue that are not available very openly, so I'm here to make sure you didn't go through the pain I have gone through 😅 so let's start.

Perquisite

Kubernetes Cluster(can create with KOps), Ingress Controller (Nginx)

Step I

Select Which chart you want to use, there are 2 helm chart

Bitnami KeyCloak
Codecentric KeyCloak

Feel Free to Use anyone of these you can just google them or click on the link provided above. For this Example we'll use the Bitnami KeyCloak, personally I think it's easier to deploy with this chart.

Step II

So I guess you decided to use the Bitnami Chart too, so there are few thing you need to take care otherwise the deployment will fail.

NOTICE

Make sure you have set the password for the external database by passing into values.yaml

externalDatabase.password

postgresql.auth.password

These 2 field should have same value otherwise you'll run into postgres error and pod will go crashback-loop
And Since we are using Nginx as Ingress-Controller we are going to to enable the ingress

ingress.enabled

ingress.hostname

ingress.pathType

I hope you are finding these value in values.yaml and overwriting them, now most Important thing since we are using Application Load Balancer in our case (I'll attach the link how to do that too soon.)
I have configure it in such a way that Before ALB all traffic is in HTTPS and from there in HTTP if you have the same case
make sure you have done this change.

proxy: edge

And You can configure the username and its password as well I hope you'll find the values.

Now You can deploy the helm chart with updated values and the wait for few seconds as it will take some time grab a water bottle for yourself 🍾.

STEP III

Confirmation that it's running successfully try the kube-proxy command to proxy the port to you're local system and see if it's running if yes then we can move forward, if not 🥺 plz check the configuration that you have made or feel free to ask in comments.

STEP IV

If you have done this step while setting up the ingress it's well and good but if not you are like me😊.

KeyCloak needs some headers to work behind proxy as it's mentioned here

We need to configure our Nginx Ingress Controller to pass the headers so after digging for 5 Days I found this,
We need to create a configmap which contains the following data

kind: ConfigMap apiVersion: v1 metadata: name: <chart-name-with-which-deployed>-nginx-ingress-controller namespace: <namespace-in-which-deployed-nginx-ingress-controller> data: use-forwarded-headers: "true" forwarded-for-header: "X-Forwarded-For"

and make sure the name is correct otherwise it will not work, to verify it's working see the logs of the pod

nginx-controller-nginx-ingress-controller

You'll see something like

Found the configmap needed to reload backend, reload complete

not exactly but something like this and you're done

Now go to your hostname that associated with keycloak you'll be able to access the admin-panel without issue.

Let's Discuss the Error if These Steps are not Completed

First if you didn't set the password whenever you'll upgrade the helm chart you'll loose the connection with postgres as the by default password is randomly generated it will change after upgrade so make sure you have provided the password.

Second if the header are not making through Ingress You'll not be able to access the admin console rather than you'll be stuck with

/admin/master/console

if it's already configure you'll not face this error.

Third too many redirect

This is due the proxy=passthrough which lead to this error.
And its default value so make sure if your tls terminate at loadbalancer or proxy which is in front of keycloak then you have to use the

proxy: edge

and it will start working

And

My Friend if you have done all this right you will be able to see the login screen of admin console

Feel Free to ask any question
item.author: ashutosh5786

item.title: 奇迹早晨：成功的 6 个早晨习惯 | The Miracle Morning: 6 Morning Habits to be Successful
item.description:
照片由Sergei A在Unsplash上拍摄

我正在听 Hal Elrod 的播客。 他解释了6个可以改变一个人的杀手习惯。 一旦我在日常生活中应用了其中的一些规则，我确信它会完美运行。

储蓄者

为当天的目标设定心态。 当您保持沉默时，您也可以使用计时器。 不要阅读，不要听任何东西，只是坐着思考。 在放松和警觉之间找到平衡的姿势。 这十分钟将使您的一天变得更有效率和更有成效，而不仅仅是忙碌的一天！

对你愿意实现的事情持肯定态度。 想想你承诺要做的事情。 你可以致力于每天跑步，或者成为百万富翁，或者储蓄 50%，或者进入一家大公司。 提醒自己你所承诺的。 在肯定的同时，想想这四点；

  1. 你致力于什么？
  2. 为什么它对你有深刻的意义？
  3. 您致力于开展哪些活动以取得成功？
  4. 您具体何时致力于开展这些活动？

这将提高意识并使您成为真正的实干家。

想象自己以极大的动力做这些活动。 想象自己穿着鞋子出门。 并想象自己以极大的动力奔跑。 想象一下自己付出了所有的努力，最终得到了成就的回报。 所有伟大的运动员都想象自己正在努力工作并实现自己的目标。

早上锻炼，即使是六十秒。 即使你晚上去健身房，很短的运动时间也会让你精力充沛，让你开始新的一天。 有许多应用程序可让您在家锻炼。 一个例子是“七分钟锻炼”。

我们所有人都处于某种状态。 有的想要幸福，有的想要富有，有的想要成为更好的人。 每个主题都有数百本书。 如果您每天阅读五页，那么每月将累积到 150 页。 如果你坚持这个习惯，这最终会让你变成一个更好的自己。

这是一种提高意识和利用你的正能量的方法。 早上只需5分钟。 您可以在应用程序或纸上书写。

  1. 写下三件让你心存感激的新事物
  2. 实现最大目标需要做的三件事

每天把它们写下来，你会发现自己有很多快乐的理由。 你在生活中取得了很多成就，而你的下一个目标只是另一个目标。 当你意识到这一点时，你会更有信心。

对自己所处的位置和所做的事情感到满意。 只要知道你会在正确的时刻达到你的目标。 所以不要感到压力或悲伤。 享受你的一天，并知道你正在逐渐实现你的目标。



原文:

Photo by Sergei A on Unsplash

I was listening to a podcast by Hal Elrod. He explained 6 killer habits that would transform a person. Once I was applying a few of those rules in my daily life and I know for sure that, it works perfectly.

SAVERS

S - Silence (Meditation, Tefekkür). Set the mindset for the goals of the day. As you are being silent, you can use a timer too. Don't read, don't listen to anything, just sit and think. Find a balanced posture between relaxation and alertness. These ten minutes will transform your day to be more effective and more productive, not just another busy day!

A - Affirmation. Be affirmative about the things that you are willing to achieve. Think of the things that you are committed to doing. You could be committed to running every day or to becoming a millionaire or saving 50% or entering a big company. Remind yourself what you are committed to. While being affirmative, think about these four points;

  1. What are you committed to?
  2. Why is it deeply meaningful to you?
  3. What activities are you committed to doing that will lead to success?
  4. When specifically are you committed to doing those activities?

This will raise awareness and make you a real doer.

V - Visualize. Visualize yourself doing the activities with great motivation. Imagine yourself wearing shoes and going outside. And imagine yourself running with great motivation. Imagine yourself doing all the hard work and finally being rewarded with achievements. All the great athletes visualize themselves working hard and achieving their goals.

E - Exercise. Exercise in the morning even if it is for sixty seconds. Even if you go to the gym in the evening, a very short period of exercise will energize you and give you a great start to the day. There are many apps that let you exercise from home. One example would be "Seven minutes Exercise".

R - Read. All of us are in some conditions. Some want to be happy, some want to be wealthy, and some want to be a better person. And there are hundreds of books on each topic. If you read five pages a day, that will accumulate to 150 pages a month. If you persist in this habit, this will eventually transform you into a better version of yourself.

S - Scribing (Writing). It is a way to raise awareness and harness positive energy in you. It will take only 5 minutes in the morning. You can write on an app or on a paper.

  1. Write three new things that you are grateful for
  2. Three important things that you need to do to achieve your biggest goal

Write them every day, and you will discover that you have so many reasons to be happy. You have achieved so many things in your life, and your next goal is just yet another goal. You would be more confident as you realize that.

Be happy with where you are and what you are doing. Just know that you will reach your goal at the right moment. So don't get stressed or sad. Just enjoy your day and know that gradually you are reaching your goals.
item.author: sadiqur_rahman

item.title: 蓝牙控制机器人 | Bluetooth Controlled Robot
item.description:
罗希思 ND

发表于在这篇博客中，我将向您展示如何使用 HC-05 蓝牙模块和 Arduino 微控制器构建蓝牙控制机器人。 您可以使用 Playstore 中提供的任何蓝牙移动应用程序将您的手机连接到蓝牙模块。

所需组件

  • 齿轮马达
  • 集成电路 L293D

为此，请使用 Arduino 编程语言（基于布线）和基于处理的 Arduino 软件 (IDE)。

您可以使用此模块在两个微控制器（例如 Arduino）之间进行通信，或与任何支持蓝牙的设备（例如电话或笔记本电脑）进行通信。 许多 Android 应用程序已经可用，这使得这个过程变得更加容易。

齿轮马达

它是一种带有齿轮箱的直流电动机，用于降低速度并增加扭矩和功率。 这种类型的电机通常用于机器人应用。

集成电路 L293D

在那个蓝牙应用程序中，点击0会关闭电机，而按下1会导致电机 1 向右旋转。 与此类似，当敲击2和3时，电机4分别向左转、电机2向右转和电机2向左转。

代码

int motorPin1 = 3 ; int motorPin2 = 4 ; int motorPin3 = 5 ; int motorPin4 = 6 ; int state ; int flag ; void setup () { Serial . begin ( 9600 ); pinMode ( 3 , OUTPUT ); pinMode ( 4 , OUTPUT ); pinMode ( 5 , OUTPUT ); pinMode ( 6 , OUTPUT ); } void loop () { if ( Serial . available () > 0 ) { state = Serial . read (); flag = 0 ; } if ( state == '0' ) { digitalWrite ( 3 , LOW ); digitalWrite ( 4 , LOW ); digitalWrite ( 5 , LOW ); digitalWrite ( 6 , LOW ); if ( flag = 0 ) { Serial . println ( "MOTOR:off" ); flag = 1 ; } } else if ( state == '1' ) { digitalWrite ( 3 , LOW ); digitalWrite ( 4 , HIGH ); if ( flag == 0 ) { Serial . println ( "MOTOR1:right" ); flag = 1 ; } } else if ( state == '2' ) { digitalWrite ( 3 , HIGH ); digitalWrite ( 4 , LOW ); if ( flag == 0 ) { Serial . println ( "MOTOR1:left" ); flag = 1 ; } } else if ( state == '3' ) { digitalWrite ( 5 , LOW ); digitalWrite ( 6 , HIGH ); if ( flag == 0 ) { Serial . println ( "MOTOR2:right" ); flag = 1 ; } } else if ( state == '4' ) { digitalWrite ( 5 , HIGH ); digitalWrite ( 6 , LOW ); if ( flag == 0 ) { Serial . println ( "MOTOR2:LEFT" ); flag = 1 ; } } }

蒂卡姆辛格阿尔玛 - 一旦暂停，ndrohith 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，ndrohith 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，ndrohith 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 ndrohith 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 您可以执行以下操作来标记 ndrohith：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 ndrohith 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Rohith ND

Posted on Aug 7

In this blog, I will show you how to build a bluetooth controlled robot with HC-05 bluetooth module and an Arduino microcontroller. You can use any bluetooth mobile app available in playstore to connect you mobile to bluetooth module.

Components required

  • Arduino UNO
  • HC-05
  • Gear Motors
  • IC L293D

Arduino UNO

Arduino is an opensource electronics platform based on easy-to-use hardware and software. Arduino boards are able to read inputs - light on a sensor , a finger on a button , or a twitter message - and turn it into an output - activating a motor , turning on a LED , publishing something online. To do so use the Arduino programming language (based on wiring) and the Arduino Software (IDE) based on processing.

HC-05 Bluetooth module

The HC-05 is a popular wireless module that can add two-way (full-duplex) functionality to your projects. You can use this module to communicate between two microcontrollers, such as Arduino, or to communicate with any Bluetooth-enabled device, such as a phone or laptop. Many Android applications are already available, making this process much easier.

Gear Motors

It is a DC motor with a gear box for decreasing the speed and increasing the torque and power . This type of motors is commonly used for robotic applications.

IC L293D

IC L293D is a dual H-bridge motor driver integrated circuit that can drive current of up to 600mA with voltage range of 4.5 to 36 volts.

In that Bluetooth app, tapping 0 turns the motor off, while pressing 1 causes the motor 1 to spin right. Similar to this, when tapping 2 , 3 and 4, the motors 1 turns left , the motor 2 turn right and the motor 2 spins left, respectively.

Code 

int motorPin1 = 3;
int motorPin2 = 4;
int motorPin3 = 5;
int motorPin4 = 6;
int state;
int flag;
void setup() { Serial.begin(9600); pinMode(3, OUTPUT); pinMode(4, OUTPUT); pinMode(5, OUTPUT); pinMode(6, OUTPUT);
} void loop() { if (Serial.available() > 0) { state = Serial.read(); flag = 0; } if (state == '0') { digitalWrite(3, LOW); digitalWrite(4, LOW); digitalWrite(5, LOW); digitalWrite(6, LOW ); if (flag = 0) { Serial.println("MOTOR:off"); flag = 1; } } else if (state == '1') { digitalWrite(3, LOW); digitalWrite(4, HIGH); if (flag == 0) { Serial.println("MOTOR1:right"); flag = 1; } } else if (state == '2') { digitalWrite(3, HIGH); digitalWrite(4, LOW ); if (flag == 0) { Serial.println("MOTOR1:left"); flag = 1; } } else if (state == '3') { digitalWrite(5, LOW); digitalWrite(6, HIGH); if (flag == 0) { Serial.println("MOTOR2:right"); flag = 1; } } else if (state == '4') { digitalWrite(5, HIGH); digitalWrite(6, LOW ); if (flag == 0) { Serial.println("MOTOR2:LEFT"); flag = 1; } }
}

Tikam Singh Alma - Jul 23

item.title: 7️⃣ 开发者必备的 VSCODE 快捷键 Thread🧵 | 7️⃣ VSCODE Shortcuts for Developers you should know Thread🧵
item.description:
本提尔沙德拉克

发表于将 CLASS attr 添加到 html 标记

div.header 👇 <div class="header"></div>
 form#search.info 👇 <form id="search" class="info"></form>
  • 添加多个 CLASS 和 ID attr
 p.info1.info2.info3#info <p class="info1 info2 info3" id='info'></p>
  1. 带孩子和乘法的元素
  • 将子元素添加到父元素
ul>li 👇 <ul> <li></li> </ul>
  • 添加多个子元素
ul>li*5 👇 <ul> <li></li> <li></li> <li></li> <li></li> <li></li> </ul>
  1. 元素与兄弟
    • 在另一个旁边添加 html 元素
 div+section+p 👇 <div></div> <section></section> <p></p>
 a{Google} 👇 <header> <a href="">Google</a> </header>
 div>(header>ul>li*2>a)+footer>p 👇 <div> <header> <ul> <li><a href=""></a></li> <li><a href=""></a></li> </ul> </header> <footer> <p></p> </footer> </div>
  1. 隐式标签名称
    • 添加具有相同类的多个子元素
 ul>.item👇 <ul> <li class="item"></li> </ul>
 table>.row>.col*3 👇 <table> <tr class="row"> <td class="col"></td> <td class="col"></td> <td class="col"></td> </tr> </table>
  1. 项目编号
  2. 将编号的类名添加到子元素
ul>li.item$*5 <ul> <li class="item1"></li> <li class="item2"></li> <li class="item3"></li> <li class="item4"></li> <li class="item5"></li> </ul>

你还能建议什么其他的 VSCODE 快捷方式？
在下方评论👇👇

一旦暂停，qbentil 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，qbentil 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，qbentil 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 qbentil 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。

感谢您保护 DEV 社区的安全。 以下是标记 qbentil 的方法：

举报其他不当行为

取消标记 qbentil 将恢复其帖子的默认可见性。



原文:

Bentil Shadrack

Posted on Aug 7 

div#info 👇
<div id="info"></div>
  • Add a CLASS attr to html tag 
div.header 👇
<div class="header"></div>

form#search.info 👇
<form id="search" class="info"></form>
  • Add multiple CLASS and ID attr 
p.info1.info2.info3#info
<p class="info1 info2 info3" id='info'></p>
  1. ELEMENT WITH CHILD and MULTIPLICATION
  • Add child element to a parent element 
ul>li 👇
<ul>
    <li></li>
</ul>
  • Add multiple child elements 
ul>li*5 👇
<ul>
    <li></li>
    <li></li>
    <li></li>
    <li></li>
    <li></li>
</ul>
  1. ELEMENT with SIBLINGS
    • Add html element next to another 
div+section+p 👇
<div></div>
<section></section>
<p></p>

a{Google} 👇
<header> <a href="">Google</a> </header>

div>(header>ul>li*2>a)+footer>p 👇
<div>
    <header>
        <ul>
            <li><a href=""></a></li>
            <li><a href=""></a></li>
        </ul>
    </header>
    <footer>
        <p></p>
    </footer>
</div>
  1. IMPLICIT TAG NAMES
    • Adding multiple child element with same class 
ul>.item👇
<ul>
    <li class="item"></li>
</ul>

table>.row>.col*3 👇
<table>
    <tr class="row">
        <td class="col"></td>
        <td class="col"></td>
        <td class="col"></td>
    </tr>
</table>
  1. ITEM NUMBERING
  2. Adding numbered class names to child elements 
ul>li.item$*5
<ul>
    <li class="item1"></li>
    <li class="item2"></li>
    <li class="item3"></li>
    <li class="item4"></li>
    <li class="item5"></li>
</ul>

What other VSCODE shortcut can you suggest?
comment below👇👇
